title: Heading component — semantic h1–h6 scale
description: Heading primitive for React pages — levels map to h1–h6 with optional display scaling and tone variants.

og.title: Typography · Headings · Zentauri UI
og.description: Heading primitive for React pages — levels map to h1–h6 with optional display scaling and tone variants.

twitter.title: Typography · Headings · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Heading primitive for React pages — levels map to h1–h6 with optional display scaling and tone variants.

intro: Use Heading to mirror your outline with semantic tags while tuning visual scale via displayLevel when marketing layouts need different sizing.
useCases: Hero titles and section titles in docs.
Admin shells with predictable heading scale.
Landing pages that keep one clear h1.
Cards where visual size differs from outline rank.

faqs.question: Can heading level differ from visual size?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use displayLevel to adjust Tailwind scale classes while keeping the semantic tag from level.

faqs.question: Do headings include spacing presets?
faqs.answer: Spacing is left to layout utilities; primitives stay predictable so page shells control rhythm.

faqs.question: Are tones accessible on dark backgrounds?
faqs.answer: Defaults target slate surfaces used across Zentauri previews; verify contrast if you change surfaces.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Maps level 1–6 to native heading tags with shared tone and emphasis toggles.

scroll-m-20 aids anchor navigation without extra wrappers.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Combine tone with bold, italic, underline, or strikethrough when specs call for emphasis.

Avoid skipping heading ranks inside the same container.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Expose the correct level for screen reader outlines.

Do not style plain divs as headings—use the semantic tag.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Import Heading from `@zentauri-ui/zentauri-components/ui/typography`.

Keep interactive playgrounds in client components only when needed.
